title: Diagnosing From a Distance Mental Illness in Eva
display_title: Diagnosing From a Distance Mental Illness in Eva
wikitext:
[]: Diagnosing From a Distance: Mental Illness in Eva Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Thu Nov 17, 2011 5:30 pm OK, I'm sure this topic has been brought up a lot, but a fresh thread can't hurt anything. =][= As I'm sure you all know, Anno apparently read up on a lot of psychology textbooks when developing Eva, and it's almost certain that as a result many Eva characters have various mental illnesses from said sources. The question is, who has them? And what do they have? Also, keep in mind that this isn't meant to be condemnation of the characters at all. Indeed, it is meant to be quite the opposite. =][= First up, Asuka, who almost certainly has a condition called borderline personality disorder. What is BDP? In a word: insidious. Here's my notes on the subject . . . SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Diagnosing From a Distance: Mental Illness in Eva Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 17, 2011 6:15 pm Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: OK, I'm sure this topic has been brought up a lot, but a fresh thread can't hurt anything.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 17, 2011 6:31 pm Yeah, we've done this several times. Rei = schizoid, Asuka = borderline/hystrionic (I favor the former), Shinji = avoidant. There are many relevant threads on the topic.

[]: Post by KingXanaduu » Thu Nov 17, 2011 6:38 pm ^ I've always thought that Misato fits more of the description of having a Histronic Personality Disorder, considering her obvious flirtatious nature. Asuka does have the attention-seeking aspect of that disorder. But I've always pegged her as having Narcissistic Personality Disorder, considering her exaggerated (sometimes Justified) sense of self-importance. @Bageera: I think another personality disorder that would fit with Misato, Shinji, and Asuka pretty well would be PTSD, we all could agree on that one.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 17, 2011 6:49 pm xanderkh wrote: I've always thought that Misato fits more of the description of having a Histronic Personality Disorder, considering her obvious flirtatious nature. Asuka does have the attention-seeking aspect of that disorder. But I've always pegged her as having Narcissistic Personality Disorder, considering her exaggerated (sometimes Justified) sense of self-importance.

[]: Re: Diagnosing From a Distance: Mental Illness in Eva Post by ReiAyanami25 » Sun Nov 20, 2011 3:54 am Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: Borderline personality disorder >Unhappy >Deep-seated sense of loneliness and emptiness > Demonize and idolize the same person (“I hate you, don't leave me!”) > Intense anger >Extremely manipulative to keep people engaged. >Intentionally or unintentionally creates crises. >Often involved with drugs, alcohol, self-scarification, eating disorders, and promiscuous sex > Can be cured with years of work

[]: Post by Trajan » Sun Nov 20, 2011 4:40 pm A lot of mental illnesses seem to be closely related to each other. In the case of Asuka, Borderline Personality Disorder and Narcissistic Personality Disorder are both valid diagnoses based on what we've seen.

[]: Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Nov 20, 2011 5:50 pm I always thought that Shinji best fit the avoidant personality, he still wants to be loved/acknowledged by others. "Gendo,cough,cough." However since he remains fearful of initial contact it becomes nearly impossible for him to explore any kind of relationship with anyone besides kaworu, and lets face it the only reason Shinji's even able to do so is because he seems like an open book at first. Asuka on the other hand is far more interesting. Here we have a character who is mostly ignored by the adults near the end of the show. So I'm not surprised that Asuka fits best into the borderline personality, just look at her interactions with Shinji and Kaji.

[]: Post by Allemann » Sun Nov 20, 2011 6:35 pm People, you're forgetting that having a mental illness makes you unable to properly function in daily life. The characters, despite many of their shortcomings, still wake up and go do their daily chores including working in a high stress environment like the Nerv base that is in active duty. The only character that is a arguable case is Asuka. This is seen when she's found naked in a bathtub in a dilapidated house. A reaction as this is a possible indication of a complication. The plot hole appears when she's still being held in the base and later deployed instead of sent for psychiatric evaluation. Another appears when one asks what happened with the standard psychological screening that is strangely absent from the U.N. that exists so that cases like Asuka never go near a military workplace.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Nov 20, 2011 6:40 pm We have a few threads on this subject already, take your pick: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6093/Evangelion-and-Personality-Disorders/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11636/EoE-Shinjis-Psychological-State/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11216/Non-Freudian-Psychology/
